channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: Evaluación de solvencia: la clave para una concesión de crédito responsable
item.description: La transparencia, la evaluación de solvencia y la información clara sobre costes son piezas clave para reducir el riesgo de sobreendeudamiento y reforzar la protección del cliente en la contratación de crédito. El crédito al consumo está bajo una vigilancia regulatoria más intensa.

item.title: Sixfold Launches AI Underwriter
item.description: (Image source: Sixfold homepage.) Sixfold (New York) has launched AI Underwriter, an underwriting agent for property/casualty insurers that assesses submissions, checks missing information, evaluates appetite and portfolio fit, and recommends next actions for underwriters. “What does a great underwriter actually need the moment they receive a submission?” comments Alex Schmelkin, Founder and CEO, Sixfold. Instead, our users told us that what they need is to understand the risk in the full context of their current book, the broker, and the guidelines, and to be presented with a clear next step.” Carrier-Specific Underwriting Context Sixfold says AI Underwriter is built on an underwriting model trained on professional standards, underwriter reasoning patterns and a curated library across lines and industries.

item.title: Origami Adds Insurance Program Management Tools
item.description: (Image source: Origami Risk website.) Origami Risk (Chicago) has introduced Insurance Program Management capabilities within its Risk Management Information System (RMIS) solution. The release describes insurance program management as an area where data is often spread across PDFs, spreadsheets, emails and broker systems. “Risk teams are skilled at managing complex insurance programs,” comments Peter Richard, Director of Product Management, Origami Risk.

item.title: Homeowner Claims Support Platform Tugboat Closes $3M Seed Round
item.description: Tugboat, a consumer insurance platform that helps homeowners with property damage and insurance claims, announced an oversubscribed $3 million in seed funding to scale its platform and offer expanded claim support for homeowners. The Simi Valley-based company said the funding will support: building a fully integrated platform with API connections to major property management systems; accelerating embedded business-to-business distribution through partnerships with multifamily property owners, lenders, and restoration networks; and sustaining Tugboat’s free disaster-response program, which activates during designated disaster events with no membership required. The round was led by ResilienceVC with participation from South Dakota First, gener8tor, Sure Ventures, Acumen America and other investors.

item.title: U.S. High Court Rebuffs Challenge to New York Law Allowing Suits Against Gun Industry
item.description: Supreme Court turned away on Monday a gun industry challenge to a New York law that permits lawsuits against gun makers, wholesalers and dealers for endangering people’s safety through sales of firearms and ammunition. The justices declined to hear an appeal by an industry trade group, the National Shooting Sports Foundation, of a lower court’s ruling upholding the law, which New York calls a public nuisance statute. The New York law, signed by former Democratic Governor Andrew Cuomo in 2021, requires the gun industry to use reasonable safeguards to protect against gun trafficking, theft, and the use of “straw purchasers” who buy firearms for someone else.

item.title: Scientists Concerned El Niño Will Be Big, Bad and Costly With Heat, Floods, Droughts, Fires
item.description: Meteorologists forecast it will rival — or exceed — a record El Niño that began in 1997 and helped trigger billions of dollars in damage from heat waves, floods, droughts, tornadoes, and wildfires. Experts said the El Niño, a natural warming cycle, should further heat a globe already warming from fossil fuel pollution and will likely turbocharge extreme weather across the planet. NOAA’s announcement said there’s a 63% chance that the El Niño will get so intense this late fall and early winter that it “would rank among the largest El Niño events in the historical record going back to 1950.” The warm, deep waters of an El Niño affect weather patterns by bringing “a lot of extra heat to the surface, fueling a lot of extreme events for a lot of places around the world,” said Clark University climate scientist Abby Frazier.

item.title: Major Gap Between Identity Security Confidence and Reality: Study
item.description: A new report uncovered a significant disconnect between enterprise confidence in identity security and operational reality, according to identity technology providers FIDO Alliance and HID. The report, “The State of Physical and Digital Identity in the Enterprise,” also found that 70% experienced at least one identity-related security incident overall. “Identity security is no longer just an authentication challenge; it is an enterprise governance challenge.

item.title: Augment Risk strengthens ILS team with Brad Livingston hire, targets private ILS expansion
item.description: Augment Risk, the risk capital and reinsurance solutions broking firm, has hired well-known insurance-linked securities market executive Brad Livingston, who joins the firm to strengthen its ILS team and in particular is expected to help grow the private ILS offering in property risk, given his collateralized reinsurance expertise. Livingston joins Augment Risk as a partner within its ILS team, effective immediately. At Augment Risk, Livingston will be tasked with expanding the ILS offering at the broker, in particular around private ILS and collateralized reinsurance.

item.title: Stone Point raises $610.5m in first close of new Insurance Solutions Fund
item.description: Stone Point Capital LLC, the private equity and credit investment firm with an insurance and reinsurance specialism, has successfully raised $610.5 million in a first close for a new strategy, named the Stone Point Insurance Solutions Fund. In the case of this new strategy, these first close commitments totalling $610.5 million have been made to a Cayman Islands structure named the Stone Point Insurance Solutions Fund, L.P. Given the lack of information, we cannot confirm that the above is the precise mandate for deployment of this new Stone Point Insurance Solutions Fund.

item.title: Aon’s White Rock issues $14.85m Raptor Re 2026-1 private cat bond
item.description: A new private catastrophe bond has come to market via an Aon operated White Rock Insurance entity that we believe to be domiciled in Guernsey, with a $14.85 million tranche of Raptor Re 2026-1 notes privately placed with qualified investors. The White Rock Insurance platform is managed by Aon Insurance Managers, so that unit will have played a role in getting this Raptor Re 2026-1 private cat bond, or cat bond lite, to market. Insurance and reinsurance broking group Aon has been utilising its range of White Rock entities for the structuring and issuance of private catastrophe bond and collateralized reinsurance arrangements for around two decades.

item.title: Data centre boom opens ‘considerable opportunity’ for US P&C insurers: AM Best
item.description: A new AM Best report has suggested that the growing proliferation of data centre projects presents a “considerable opportunity” for the U.S. Similar dynamics were highlighted in a recent report from S&P Global on hyperscale data centre campuses , which found construction-phase total insured values reaching $20bn–$50bn as markets adjust to a step-change in asset scale, pointing to a growing but increasingly selective opportunity for re/insurers. David Blades, associate director, Industry Research and Analytics, AM Best, commented, “As data centre development and construction spreads, the required insurance coverage is evolving, as it is currently beyond what the traditional property/casualty industry has previously experienced.” The rating agency’s report noted that “hyperscale” data centres are currently attracting the most attention and scrutiny due to their scale, rapid expansion, and significant effects on local communities.

item.title: Hippo Holdings names Laura Boettcher as Chief Operating Officer for group-wide operations
item.description: Hippo Holdings Inc., the US-based parent company of Hippo Insurance, has announced the promotion of Laura Boettcher, ARe, to Chief Operating Officer. Rick McCathron, President and Chief Executive Officer of Hippo Holdings, commented on the appointment, saying, “Laura has been an invaluable part of Hippo’s leadership team for nearly five years, and she has earned the trust of our executive council and board of directors,” said Rick McCathron, President and CEO of Hippo Holdings. She had been serving as Chief Operating Officer of Hippo Insurance since February 2024, where the company says she was responsible for core operational activity while balancing service delivery and business requirements.

item.title: Juan Naveda to lead Dynamic Re as CEO, London
item.description: Dynamic Reinsurance, a firm specialising in tailored reinsurance risk transfer solutions, has appointed Juan Naveda as Chief Executive Officer (CEO), London. Naveda has over three decades of insurance and reinsurance industry experience, and brings extensive expertise and strong relationships across the London market. The reinsurer, in a statement, commented on the appointment, “Juan’s technical expertise, market insight, and leadership will be instrumental as we continue to strengthen our capabilities and advance Dynamic Reinsurance’s international growth strategy.

item.title: Florida Tries to Block Rescue Pets from New Mexico and Texas as Screwworm Spreads
item.description: The Florida Department of Agriculture and Consumer Services Pets has barred pets rescued from shelters in Texas and New Mexico in a beefed-up effort to stop the spread of the New World Screwworm pest that infests livestock. Related: Screwworm Parasite Spreads Beyond Texas Texas Governor Vows Sped Up Construction of Screwworm Fly Breeding Facility The emergency rule, seen here , expands restrictions on animal imports from affected areas, prohibits the importation of rescue and shelter dogs and cats from states with confirmed screwworm detections, and revises the definition of infested zones to any county with a confirmed screwworm detection and its surrounding counties, the statement noted.

item.title: Missouri Sues Baby Monitor Manufacturer for Alleged Links to Chinese Military
item.description: The lawsuit alleges Lorex has concealed material ties to the Chinese military via its manufacturer, Dahua, putting families at risk. Department of Defense designating Dahua as a Chinese Military Company that threatens national security, according to the Missouri Attorney General’s Office. Missouri Attorney General Catherine Hanaway has filed a lawsuit against Lorex Corporation, a major seller of home cameras and baby monitors.

item.title: Allianz emerges as frontrunner to acquire HSBC Life Singapore in potential US$2bn deal
item.description: The post Allianz emerges as frontrunner to acquire HSBC Life Singapore in potential US$2bn deal appeared first on Insurtech Insights . German insurance giant Allianz is reportedly leading the race to acquire HSBC Life Singapore as HSBC Holdings explores strategic options for its insurance business in the city-state, according to reports citing sources familiar with the matter. For Allianz, an acquisition of HSBC Life Singapore would represent another major opportunity to strengthen its position in the Singapore market following an unsuccessful attempt to acquire a majority stake in Income Insurance in 2024.

item.title: Goosehead names Eben Hewitt as CTO
item.description: Goosehead Insurance announced the appointment of Eben Hewitt as Chief Technology Officer. Hewitt joins Goosehead from Hyatt Corporation, where he served as Global Chief Information Officer, overseeing engineering, data and AI, infrastructure, cybersecurity, technology operations, and enterprise applications. As CTO, Hewitt will lead Goosehead’s technology strategy, including the expansion of AI-powered capabilities, data and analytics platforms, and the continued evolution of Digital Agent 2.0.

item.title: Simplicity acquires August H. Velten & Associates
item.description: As part of the transaction, founder August Velten will become a Simplicity Partner, and the registered investment advisor is expected to rebrand as Simplicity Wealth Advisors: Melbourne. Simplicity Group Holdings has acquired August H. Velten & Associates, an independent retail wealth management and annuity producer based in Melbourne, Florida.

item.title: GetCovered acquires Revyse
item.description: GetCovered, a risk and compliance platform for multifamily housing operators, announced the acquisition of Revyse, a full-service vendor compliance and contract management company. The Revyse platform provides a single system to manage bids, contracts, credentials, and spend. The acquisition will complement GetCovered’s existing vendor risk and compliance capabilities with enhanced tools for contract management, spend intelligence, and AI-powered vendor compliance automation.

item.title: Prex gana autonomía en Perú con licencia de crédito y queda a un paso de obtener la de dinero electrónico
item.description: Se trata de la primera empresa de tecnología financiera que obtiene esta licencia en el país andino.“Si bien entregamos la posibilidad a un usuario de que acceda a un crédito, la verdad es que con esto [la licencia] va a tener un producto mucho mejor estructurado y vamos a disponibilizarlo a muchas más personas”, dijo a iupana Ignacio Nina, country manager andean region de Prex. Prexpe acaba de recibir la autorización de la Superintendencia de Bancos, Seguros y AFP (SBS) de Perú para operar como empresa de crédito, lo que le permite otorgar financiamiento con recursos propios. La fintech ya tiene como prioridad duplicar su cartera de créditos, mejorar sus modelos de scoring y ampliar su baraja de productos este año.

item.title: If AI Is Addictive, Where Does The Responsibility Lie—With Big Tech Or Users?
item.description: If we follow the argument that generative AI is a candidate for addictive behavior, then we also need to look at responsibility. Generative AI is not formally recognized as addictive right now – the medical evidence is still being gathered. The starting point would be to identify who carries responsibility for overuse of generative AI.

item.title: Trump Transportation Department Rescinds ‘Disparate Impact’ Civil Rights Regulation
item.description: Transportation Department said last Wednesday it is rescinding part of its long-standing civil rights regulations that prohibit conduct that has an unintended “disparate” impact. Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy said the department is making clear its regulations “prohibit only intentional discrimination, not conduct or activities that have a disparate impact,” and added USDOT will not take action on disparate-impact liability. Federal Discrimination Laws Date to Civil War On June 9, the Justice Department said the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission’s legal guidance to deter disparate impacts on protected groups of workers is wrong because it focuses solely on outcomes with no regard for an employer’s intent.

item.title: Shippers Cautious on Hormuz Strait Transit After US and Iran Agree on Deal
item.description: Shippers in Asia and Europe said confidence in resuming transit through the Strait of Hormuz could take weeks to rebuild and navigation will only restart once safety is assured, after the U.S. Shippers have welcomed news of the deal, but are still waiting for more details, including mine clearance in the strait. WAR LARGELY STOPPED SHIPPING THROUGH STRAIT The U.S.-Israeli war with Iran that began on February 28 has largely stopped shipping through the strait, the transit route for roughly a fifth of the world’s oil and liquefied natural gas supply, along with vital products such as aluminum and urea.

item.title: Making Decisions Under Pressure in Complex Risk Environments
item.description: This is leading insurers to rethink how they assess risk and make the right decisions without sacrificing speed and efficiency. “Risk is risk, and insurers are very good at addressing risk, containing it, minimizing it, especially when they think that the risk is too complex or evolving,” said Scott Seaman, attorney at Hinshaw Law, during a panel discussion at Carrier Management’s annual InsurTech Summit. Insurance leaders say today’s risk environment is creating challenges that are more difficult to predict than ever before.

item.title: VIG Re names Wilfrid Goh as chief representative for APAC
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Tysers appoints William Furness-Smith as interim Singapore CEO
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: The AI Startup Funding Boom Is Not A Global Phenomenon
item.description: The flood of AI-focused funding has pushed global startup investment to record levels this year. So far this year, nearly 88% of AI-related startup funding, or $319 billion, went to U.S.-headquartered companies, per Crunchbase data. for startup funding, a few of the larger technology investment hubs are seeing year-over-year gains.

item.title: Rewriting Your Pitch: SaaS Isn’t Dead, But The Playbook For Founders Is Changing
item.description: What this means for your pitch: A strong SaaS founder today must be able to demonstrate a sharp wedge, a clear buyer, strong usage, measurable ROI and a product roadmap that expands from point solution into platform. For decades, the SaaS playbook was clear: predictable revenue streams, very high gross margins, efficient customer acquisition and strong net revenue retention made a startup very attractive to investors. But today, with the launch of LLMs, and in the shadow of the “SaaSpocalypse,” 30 years of relative SaaS stability has been shattered and the playbook is being rewritten with disappearing ink.

item.title: Fidelis Partnership Syndicate 3123 targeted for $75m Woody Re 2026-1 cat bond via Arthur Re
item.description: platform, with a $75 million target for industry-loss triggered multi-peril reinsurance to benefit the Fidelis Partnership linked Syndicate 3123 at Lloyd’s, through this Arthur Re Ltd. This will be the first catastrophe bond to directly provide its protection to Syndicate 3123, the Lloyd’s structure launched in 2024 by the Fidelis Partnership in collaboration with capacity partner Pelagos Insurance Capital (formerly Fidelis Insurance) . The cat bond notes will provide the Fidelis Partnership linked Syndicate 3123 at Lloyd’s with a source of catastrophe reinsurance against losses from named storms, earthquakes, severe thunderstorms, winter storms and wildfire events, over a three year term to the end of June 2029, we are told.

item.title: World Bank cat bond on the table in new $400m Morocco Climate & Risk Finance Program
item.description: The Board of the World Bank has approved a new $400 million program that aims to boost Morocco’s resilience against climate, disaster, and cyber risks, with catastrophe bonds and other forms of insurance risk transfer including parametric triggers mooted as part of the program outcomes. The $400m Morocco Climate & Risk Finance Program will explore a wide-range of risk transfer instruments and structures, to find the optimal solutions to provide the necessary protection, while making use of private capital to support risk transfer limits required. While strengthening the country’s resilience against climate and natural disasters, as well as cyber events, the $400 million Morocco Climate & Risk Finance Program also looks to help unlock private capital to support climate infrastructure development.

item.title: Liberty and ICEYE team on parametric wildfire insurance using satellite data
item.description: Liberty and ICEYE, a provider of satellite data and services to inform decision-making and analysis, have partnered together to launch a new parametric wildfire insurance solution that uses satellite data to assess building-level damage. Jean-Christophe Garaix, Head of Parametrics & Agriculture at Liberty, commented: “By pushing the boundaries of technology, we are delivering a new generation of parametric wildfire coverage that is faster, more responsive, and fully data driven. Our property wildfire cover is remotely sensed and designed to address the growing threat of wildfires, providing reliable protection alongside traditional insurance that allows for more efficient claims settlement.” Anke Sielker, Head of Reinsurance Solutions at ICEYE, said: “We are delighted to be working with Liberty on this ground-breaking solution.

item.title: Clear Group CFO Tim Money to depart as Nathan Bavidge appointed successor
item.description: We thank him for his outstanding contribution and wish him every success in the future.” Clear Group confirmed that Money will be succeeded by Nathan Bavidge, who is currently chief financial officer at Three Rock Group, the parent company of Chill Insurance, Quote Devil Insurance and Ivernia MGA. Clear Group, an independent insurance intermediary and financial services group, has announced that its Chief Financial Officer, Tim Money, will leave the business at the end of June 2026 after more than six years in the role. I wish you all every success for the future.” Clear Group Chief Executive Mike Edgeley paid tribute to Money’s contribution, highlighting his impact on the organisation’s scale and financial performance.

item.title: World Bank approves $400m Morocco program to expand finance for climate, disaster, and cyber risks
item.description: The World Bank’s Board of Directors has approved the $400 million Morocco Climate & Risk Finance Program, aimed at strengthening Morocco’s financial resilience to climate, disaster, and cyber risks, while helping to unlock private capital for the country’s climate infrastructure development. According to the World Bank, the program will develop disaster and cyber insurance instruments to expand risk transfer capacity; strengthen institutional frameworks; reinforce digital payments infrastructure to accelerate the flow of finance following shocks; and build the capacity of financial regulators to oversee climate and cyber risks affecting banks and insurers. The World Bank continued, “Over the next five years, the program aims to mobilise up to $400 million in private capital, put in place $1 billion in pre-arranged disaster financing, and extend cyber risk coverage to at least 20 financial entities, whilst helping bring a new generation of climate infrastructure projects to market.

item.title: Will Moss joins QBE Re as Head of Marine, Energy & Aviation, London
item.description: QBE Re, the reinsurance division of global insurance group QBE, has announced the appointment of Will Moss as its Head of Marine, Energy & Aviation, London. In his new position, Moss will be responsible for QBE Re’s Marine, Energy and Aviation portfolio written out of the company’s London office, as the reinsurer continues to build out this book of business. The executive will be reporting to Mark Jackaman, QBE Re’s Executive Director for International, who commented: “We are pleased to welcome someone of Will’s talent to QBE Re to lead our London Marine, Energy & Aviation team.

item.title: US and Iran Say They’ve Agreed Deal to Reopen Hormuz This Week
item.description: The US and Iran said they reached an interim peace agreement to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and move further toward ending a war that’s killed thousands of people across the Middle East. Read more: Shipowners Seek Clarity on Hormuz Deal as Waiting Flotillas Grow Yet for all the relief in markets and among Middle Eastern states that got caught up in the conflict, Washington and Tehran still need to decide on the final steps related to what they’re calling a memorandum, set to be signed in Switzerland on Friday. Immediately once the MOU is signed, Iran is meant to reopen the Hormuz strait and the US to lift its blockade of Iranian ports.

item.title: Scouring the Strait of Hormuz for Mines Could Take Weeks
item.description: Ensuring the Strait of Hormuz is safe from mines could delay a return to normal shipping traffic by weeks following a deal to reopen the waterway, shipping and maritime security sources say. “The threat of mines in the area remains a concern immediately as well as further down the line and mine-free routes need to be established.” It is unclear how many mines Iran may have laid in the strait, which handled 20% of the world’s daily supply of oil and liquefied natural gas before the war. The operation by conventional minesweepers and state-of-the-art underwater drones could continue for 40 to 50 days before many insurance, shipping or oil companies are confident enough to sail through, according to assessments from five Western maritime security sources.

item.title: Baldoni, Lively Fight Ends With Actress Awarded Legal Fees But No Damages
item.description: Blake Lively can recover some legal costs from fellow actor and director Justin Baldoni but not punitive damages and other relief she sought after settling her legal claims over their 2024 film “It Ends With Us,” a judge ruled Friday. Liman said in a written decision that Lively can recover legal fees and costs related to her defense against a countersuit Baldoni brought against her after she sued him in December 2024. Prior Knowledge Ends Filmmaker Baldoni’s Coverage for Harassment Claims: Insurer Liman said an exception would be if Baldoni and his production company, Wayfarer Studios LLC, could prove malice fueled Lively’s claims, but that Baldoni and Wayfarer had produced no evidence to show that.

item.title: APRIL launches motorcycle insurance quotes in ChatGPT
item.description: French insurance broker APRIL Group has launched APRIL Moto, an application within ChatGPT that allows users to obtain motorcycle insurance quotes through a conversational interface. With APRIL Moto in ChatGPT, we are opening a new distribution channel that complements and strengthens our ecosystem of brokers and partners. The experience collects information about the customer, vehicle, and coverage needs before connecting to APRIL Moto’s pricing engines to generate a quote based on actual underwriting criteria.

item.title: Philippines quake claims expected to escalate, testing local claims management, loss adjusters say
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Asia’s protection gap remains stubbornly persistent as new exposures remain uninsured
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: An 84-year reign came to an end, and more
item.description: Liberty Mutual launched a conversational AI quoting app inside ChatGPT — the first carrier-backed integration of its kind — initially in seven states with plans to reach more than 40 by year-end. State Farm’s own Q1 2026 results confirmed a different turnaround : its P&C carriers swung from a $5.1 billion underwriting loss a year ago to a $1.8 billion gain, with auto going from -$3.6 billion to +$1 billion. Progressive, meanwhile, posted a 90.2% combined ratio for April , reported $1.1 billion in net income for the month, expanded its Snapshot telematics model to 14 states , and spent a record $1.5 billion on advertising in Q1 — up 20% year over year.

item.title: ITC Infotech and InsureMO Partner to Bring AI-Driven Insurance Modernisation to Middle East, Africa, and India
item.description: The post ITC Infotech and InsureMO Partner to Bring AI-Driven Insurance Modernisation to Middle East, Africa, and India appeared first on Coverager - Insurance news and insights . Rajat Sharma, Chief Revenue Officer at InsureMO , said: “ITC Infotech’s strength in the Middle East and India, combined with their K-Fabric AI framework, makes them an ideal partner to bring our platform to carriers who want to modernise without disruption. Our alliance with InsureMO provides a platform for our K-Fabric AI agents to operate natively across the insurance value chain, helping carriers deploy intelligent automation faster, with lower risk and cost than traditional transformation programmes.” The alliance is effective immediately, with joint go-to-market activities planned across the Middle East, India, and Africa in 2026.

item.title: TCS to deploy Claude across insurance, banking, and healthcare
item.description: Anthropic has partnered with Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) to expand the use of Claude across regulated industries, including insurance, financial services, healthcare, and the public sector. Under the agreement, TCS will roll out Claude to 50,000 employees across 56 countries and launch a dedicated practice focused on designing, implementing, and managing Claude-based systems for enterprise clients. The company also plans to develop industry-specific solutions powered by Claude, including claims processing applications for insurers and lending advisory tools for banks.

item.title: Barclays expands family banking offering with GoHenry acquisition
item.description: According to Barclays, the acquisition supports its strategy to deepen relationships with households by expanding its offering for younger customers and families. We’re excited to welcome GoHenry to Barclays, where it will turbocharge our offering for households and families. GoHenry supports our vision to offer a deep and seamless banking experience to customers through all of life’s big moments, whether opening a very first account, saving for retirement, and everything in between.” – Vim Maru, CEO of Barclays UK.

item.title: Motive Partners invests in Mobius to expand pension and wealth infrastructure
item.description: Mobius has built a distinctive platform that enables this shift – connecting asset managers, pension schemes, and wealth platforms through flexible, open architecture.” – Scott Kauffman, Founding Partner, Head of Investments at Motive Partners. Private investment firm Motive Partners has agreed to acquire UK-based pension investment platform Mobius from Phoenix Equity Partners, with the transaction expected to close later this year, subject to customary conditions. According to Motive, the investment supports its thesis around the convergence of pensions, wealth, and private markets, as demand grows for private market access, personalized investment solutions, and modern investment infrastructure.

item.title: New Zealand’s NHC grows reinsurance programme to record US$7.3bn
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Aon gives Harry Edwards new Asia growth leader role
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Foyer integrates Tractable’s AI into car claims process
item.description: Foyer has partnered with Tractable to introduce AI-powered image analysis into its motor claims process for minor incidents. By combining their industry expertise with our artificial intelligence technologies, we are helping to reinvent the claims process, making it faster, more transparent, and simpler for policyholders, while meeting the industry’s highest standards.” – Giacomo Mariotti, general manager at Tractable. The Luxembourg-based insurer will use Tractable’s technology to automatically assess vehicle damage from photos submitted by policyholders, enabling faster claims handling and more efficient case management.

item.title: Fujitsu and Daiichi Life launch quantum computing research
item.description: Fujitsu and Daiichi Life Group have launched a joint research project to explore the use of quantum technology in insurance asset management. The research will use Dai-ichi Life Insurance’s real-world asset management operations as a testing ground and evaluate performance using quantum simulators and quantum computers. Under the partnership, Fujitsu will provide expertise in quantum algorithms along with access to its quantum computing infrastructure, including a large-scale quantum simulator comprising 1,024 FX700 supercomputer nodes.

item.title: Sompo launches new environmental strategy
item.description: Sompo Holdings has launched a new sustainability strategy called “SOMPO Earth Positive Actions,” expanding its previous climate-focused framework to address climate change, biodiversity, and the circular economy through what it describes as a “synergy approach.” The Japanese insurer said the strategy aims to support both environmental progress and the group’s long-term growth, while maintaining its target of achieving net-zero emissions by 2050. Sompo said approximately 70,000 employees across the group will work with customers, business partners, and local communities to implement the strategy. The initiative is built around three focus areas: regional action, global engagement, and environmental talent development.
